ab: karuwa ku kiumaumalr kaninu?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: karuwa, wawai ku.
ch: B:可以，我很願意。
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: karuwa ku kiumaumalr kaninu?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: amanan, ’azi sazu ku kinalrazaman.
ch: B:不要，我所知道的不多。
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: miwari u nu piya kare’alri? maranger ku mukuwa muzangiya kaninu, ’inava awlra?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你，可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that OK?

ab: ai, ngangara ku kaninu i ruma’.
ch: B:好，我會在家等你。
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: miwari u nu ’emanan? maranger ku kiumalr kaninu za vativatiyan kana Pinuyumayan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於卑南族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Puyuma tribe.

ab: auwa ku i zenan nu ’emanan, iniyan ku i ruma’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: auwa ku i zenan nu ’emanan, uniyan ku i ruma’.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: kinger ku mu, salaw u malrazam zatu kakuwayanan kana Pinuyumayan zi, maranger ku kipanadam kaninu.
ch: A:我聽說您對卑南族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about Puyuma traditions and customs, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: ’azi ku kamalrazaman, mawmu, kiyarungetr ku temuvang.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya, karuwa ku pasirep semasing? nu kemani mu, kalre’ayan ku venavalray i likuzan lra.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: ai, kuwatreng za eman.
ch: B:好的，沒問題。
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya, manueman nu ’ami harem?
ch: A:請問您今年幾歲了?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: makaiwa misama’ za waluwa ku ’ami lra.
ch: B:我已經98歲了。
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: lralrak u ziya.
ch: A:您真年輕。
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: ’azi! dawdawan ku lra.
ch: B:沒有啦!我已經老了。
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya, kemay u i iyan?
ch: A:請問您來自哪裡?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: kemay ku i Pinaseki.
ch: B:我來自下賓朗。
en: B: I’m from Xia Binlang.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya, a Pinuyumayan nanu ’azin?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是卑南族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Puyuma?

ab: amelri, a Amis intaw.
ch: B:不，她是阿美族人。
en: B: No, she’s Amis.

ab: malrazam u merengay za emaeman ngaiyan?
ch: A:您會說哪些語言?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: malrazam ku temarapinuyumayan zi temaraamis.
ch: B: 我會說卑南語和阿美語。
en: B: I speak Puyuma and Amis.

ab: salaw ku semangal maruwa kiumaumalr kaninu, vavati ziya za palisiyan kana pa’alrupan maw?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ai. a lralrak ku ziya mu, marayas ku mukuwa pa’alrupa i zenan.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: lrayuwan, salaw ’inava nu ipanadam harem kana wari.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: tatena’ peniya nanku nirengay, nintaw trungutrungulran vativatiyan kana temuwamuwan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: ta palruwanay ziya i kani nanta kiumaumalran harem. lrayuwan za ’azi nu ka’itrilay ku panadam.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: ikaira, nu miwari ta mu, mazayazayara ta lra.
ch: B:不客氣，有時間我們再聊。
en: B: You’re welcome. We’ll talk again when we have time.

ab: uwaveruk ku lra, talrima ku za masalr kinger ziya za marasazu za vativatiyan.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: ai, tawar lra.
ch: B:好，請慢走。
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
